ddls:
CREATE TABLE "temperature_data" (
  "temperature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each temperature record */,
  "temperature_celsius" REAL /* Temperature measurement in Celsius */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the temperature measurement */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the temperature measurement */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the source of the temperature data */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the temperature measurement */,
  "anomaly_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the temperature measurement is an anomaly */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the temperature data */,
  "statistic_id" INTEGER /* Type of statistics (e.g., Jan Average, Feb Average) */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("temperature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_data_statistic_id FOREIGN KEY ("statistic_id") REFERENCES statistics ("statistic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_temperature_data_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Official name of the country */,
  "iso3_code" TEXT /* ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code for the country */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the country */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographical region of the country */,
  "population" TEXT /* Population of the country */,
  "area_km2" TEXT /* Area of the country in square kilometers */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statistics" (
  "statistic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type of statistic */,
  "statistic_name" TEXT /* Name of the statistic (e.g., Jan Average, Feb Average) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the statistic */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement (e.g., Celsius, Fahrenheit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("statistic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time of the user */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., weather station, satellite) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each temperature record
Reference to the source of the temperature data

Flag indicating if the temperature measurement is an anomaly
Type of statistics (e.g., Jan Average, Feb Average)
